frozen_components: base small language model (13B, for critique generation)
verifier (GPT-4 in strong setup; self-verifier in weak setup)
architecture.summary: A two-stage pipeline for training self-correction in small language models (13B). Stage 1: prompt a small LM to critique its own incorrect solutions using correct solutions as guidance, then filter critiques. Stage 2: fine-tune the small LM on (incorrect solution, critique, refined solution) tuples via supervised learning. The system uses a verifier (either GPT-4 or the model itself) to decide when to invoke correction, creating a feedback loop where the model's own generated critiques steer refinement of its reasoning.
trained_components: self-correcting reasoner (via SFT on critique-guided refinement data)
critique generation (implicit in base model, filtered before training)

biblio.title: Small Language Models Need Strong Verifiers to Self-Correct Reasoning

claims.statement: small language models (13B) can develop self-correction ability on reasoning tasks with minimal inputs from stronger LMs
improved self-correction abilities of two models on five datasets spanning math and commonsense reasoning
notable performance gains when paired with a strong GPT-4-based verifier
limitations are identified when using a weak self-verifier for determining when to correct
leverage correct solutions to guide the model in critiquing their incorrect responses
